commented_code:
TestSchema.parse(blob); // throws
 ```
 
+## Files
+
+This basic implementation validates the input type. As usual, you can set custom error messages.
+
+```ts
+const imageFile = z.file({
+  required_error: "file is required",
+  invalid_type_error: "This object must be a file",
+});
+```
+
+You can validate the file's minimum and maximum size.
+
+```ts
+const imageFile = z.file().size({ min: 100000, max: 200000 });

commented_code:
z.string().endsWith(string);
 z.string().trim(); // trim whitespace
 z.string().datetime(); // defaults to UTC, see below for options
+z.string().date(); // ISO short date format.
+z.string().time(); // time of day in 24-hour format. same options as datetime.

discussion_comments.comment_body:
Would it be useful to mention that this doesn't actually validate the correctness of a date/time, but just applies the appropriate regex? e.g. this would be valid, but obviously is still an "incorrect" date: 

```ts
z.string().datetime().parse("2020-32-01T00:00:00.123456Z");
```

and point users to the `### Datetime validation` section perhaps?
Good idea! have added this to the existing section.

Choosing more explicit wording here.

"Without UTC offset" is potentially misleading, as there is some nuance in the fact that "Z" is a zone indicator for the zero UTC offset, as opposed to the absence of an offset.
